title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 September 2024.

items.title: Steam’s improved **family sharing** is out now for everyone
bullets: The Steam Families feature lets you share games with multiple people, manage parental controls, and approve requests to buy games.

Up to five family members can be invited into a group, which will grant access to a shared family library. Each person can create their own save files and collect their own achievements for games in the family library

The marquee development with the new family feature is that multiple people within the group will be able to play games from the shared library at the same time. And if you own multiple copies of a game, then multiple people can be playing that title at once. The caveat with family sharing is that a game developer may opt not to support the feature

items.title: **Super Smash Bros**. director "so sorry" to fans playing for thousands of hours
bullets: "The time players spend in a game is a cost."

items.title: **Dead Island 2** getting zombie-infested co-op horde mode next month
bullets: In addition to this new horde mode, the forthcoming update will also include New Game Plus. This will allow players to head back to LA (or, Hell A, as the Dead Island 2 team call it) for another crack at the main story, but with an additional skill slot and an increased level cap.

This free update will be arriving on 22nd October, and will coincide with the release of Dead Island 2's Ultimate Edition. This edition will include the base game, as well as both story expansions (Haus and SoLA) and a number of weapons and skin packs

items.title: **Xbox** is bringing back friend requests
bullets: Microsoft will begin testing a new friends and followers experience next week

items.title: **Payday 3**’s director has been demoted following the game’s troubled first year
bullets: Former game director Miodrag Kovačević is now serving as a senior game designer on the project, according to his LinkedIn profile, while Andreas Häll Penninger and Almir Listo will be “the creative force behind the upcoming updates”.
Penninger was a lead producer on Payday 3 and a producer on its predecessor, while Listo is global brand director and head of community for the series.

items.title: Konami confirms **MGS Master Collection Volume 2**, is considering how to handle the ‘really big titles’
bullets: "It's going to be a little while before we get a clear picture," says Konami producer, in relation to the fact that MGS2 and 3 had pre-existing ports and the remaining games in the series do not have existing ports.

items.title: **Elden Ring**’s latest patch nerfs the final boss in Shadow of the Erdtree
bullets: Other changes include balance adjustments to PvP, equipment, weapon arts, spells, incantations and numerous fixes for bugs and performance issues.

items.title: September’s **PlayStation Plus** Game Catalogue and Classics titles revealed
bullets: All of the games listed below will be available beginning on September 17.

items.title: Nexon spins off **Dave the Diver** dev Mintrocket into a full corporation
bullets: Mintrocket will still be supported by Nexon, which wants to 'expand' the studio in the wake of Dave the Diver's massive success

The "indie" corporation's Dave the Diver was a critical and commercial darling for Nexon last year, and managed to sell 1 million copies over a week after launch. It's now sold 4 million copies, following on from the 3 million milestone in January.

items.title: **Unrecord** dev Drama Studios raises $2.5 million in seed round
bullets: The French studio plans to put its new money toward making a demo for Unrecord and grow its staff before securing a publishing partner

items.title: **Cypher Games** raises $10m in latest fundraising round
bullets: The Turkish developer says it marks a key milestone as it develops its "very first match-3 title to disrupt the mobile gaming industry."

Led by The Raine Group, the round secured funding from Play Ventures, 500 Global, Riccardo Zacconi (founder of King), Humam Sakhnini (former vice-chairman of Activision Blizzard) and Akin Babayigit (co-founder of Tripledot).

items.title: Studio co-founded by **Dr Disrespect** confirms ‘significant’ layoffs
bullets: Job losses at Midnight Society come months after the streamer was fired
No specific number of job losses published, but the term "significant" was brought up
